penalty_extra_disagreement.analysis: Seventh video (V20, V22, V26, V28, V29, V30, V31) where Claude applies an out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty. GPT-4o's rubric-strict 2.5 is the correct rubric application. The pattern is now stable across catastrophe, recovery, and improvement runs and across all three equipment configurations — definitively a prompt-level scoring drift on Claude's side, not a video-quality artifact.

phase_attribution_disagreement.claude_diagnosis: Phase split: load 5s, placement 18s, throws 70s (25+17+28), cut 47s. Cutting is the dominant loss center at 34% of total time.
phase_attribution_disagreement.gpt4o_diagnosis: Phase split: needle_load 7.4s, placement 34.9s, first 22.6s, second 25.9s, third 31.9s, cut 16.7s. Placement and throws are the dominant loss centers; cut is fast.
phase_attribution_disagreement.analysis: Same INVERSE phase-split pattern as V30. Endpoint delta is only 2.6s but the cut/throws boundary differs by ~30s. Claude's cut phase always runs ~30-50s longer than GPT-4o's on direct-camera runs because Claude defines cut start at 'visible scissors action' while GPT-4o defines it at 'tails presented for cutting.' V28 mirror also confirmed: same pattern in opposite direction. Suggested fix unchanged: pin cut phase start to first scissors entry into the field.

fls_score_reconciliation.reconciliation_note: TIGHTEST score agreement in the entire 32-video series at delta 0.1 FLS — effectively exact. Reconciled value is 457.9 (GPT-4o, rubric-strict). The 0.1-point delta is the residual after Claude's 2.5-point out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty offsets Claude's 2.6-second time advantage. This is the strongest two-teacher calibration anchor in the dataset and validates the rubric-correct score for V31.

systematic_findings.tightest_score_agreement_in_series: FLS delta 0.1 is the tightest non-coincidental agreement recorded (V28 hit exact 0.0 by canceling deltas; V30 hit 1.3; V31 now 0.1 with offsetting deltas of similar magnitude). Direct-camera recordings consistently produce single-digit FLS gaps.
systematic_findings.rubric_scope_issue_now_definitive: Seventh out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty case from Claude. Pattern is stable across (a) catastrophe runs (V28, V29), (b) recovery runs (V30), (c) improvement runs (V31), (d) all three equipment configurations, and (e) all three recording-quality tiers. Definitively a prompt drift, not a video artifact. Recommend formalizing the rubric-allowed penalty set in v002 of the scoring prompt and adding a separate cosmetic_quality field if Claude's signal is worth retaining.
systematic_findings.inverse_phase_boundary_pattern_confirmed: V30 and V31 form a stable pair on the cut-phase boundary inversion: both runs show GPT-4o cut <25s and Claude cut >45s, with the difference flowing into placement and throws on the GPT-4o side. V28 mirror confirmed in the opposite direction. The cut phase start definition is the single highest-impact prompt fix available.
systematic_findings.drain_agreement_continues: V31 is another drain-closure agreement case. Running tally: AGREE 11 (V12, V13, V18, V19, V22, V26, V27, V28, V29, V30, V31), DIVERGE 5 (V11, V14, V16, V17, V20).
systematic_findings.blue_trainer_adaptation_validated: Both teachers independently confirm V30→V31 improvement. Claude: 158→137 (13%). GPT-4o: 161.8→139.6 (14%). Identical adaptation curve from independent scoring. Compare to Vizio adaptation V28→V29: Claude 365→295 (19%), GPT-4o 370→304.5 (18%). Both stations show measurable improvement on attempt 2, but blue trainer reaches usable performance immediately (sub-160s on attempt 1 vs 365s for Vizio).
systematic_findings.v28_through_v31_arc_complete: All four transition videos now have both Claude and GPT-4o scores plus reconciled comparisons. The full equipment-narrative is independently corroborated by two teachers across both pre-catastrophe (V27 = 142s/RCA), catastrophe (V28 = 365-370s/Vizio first attempt), adaptation (V29 = 295-304.5s/Vizio second attempt), recovery (V30 = 158-161.8s/blue trainer first attempt), and improvement (V31 = 137-139.6s/blue trainer second attempt). The dataset now contains the strongest two-teacher equipment-effect signal in the series.
